description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a complex hip roof covered in dark brown shingles. The exterior walls are finished in a combination of beige and brown tones, with stone or brickwork at the base and around the entryway. Large windows with black railings and trim are symmetrically arranged on the front and sides, providing ample natural light. The front features a covered porch with steps leading up to the main entrance, and there are additional covered balconies or porches on the sides. The house sits on a raised foundation with a stone or brick base, and the roof includes a small chimney. The overall design blends modern and traditional elements, giving it a contemporary suburban appearance.
description: A two-story suburban house with a dark hip roof, beige and brown exterior, large windows, and a covered front porch with steps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with steps, a covered porch, large windows, and a prominent hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, uninterrupted wall, smaller windows, and the full expanse of the hip roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary covered porch, large windows, and the back section of the roof with chimney visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view featuring a tall vertical window, side porches, and the extended hip roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large windows and vertical strips
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, single main door, stone or brick accents, and overhanging roof
